scan_note: Second scan of the day. bioRxiv API blocked by egress proxy. Used web search to find papers not captured in earlier scans. These include papers outside the 3-day window that were missed previously.

papers.title: Hematogenous neuroinvasion and genotype-dependent transmission of influenza A H5N1 viruses in the cat host
papers.abstract: H5N1 clade 2.3.4.4b viruses (B3.13 and D1.1 genotypes) characterized for pathogenesis and transmissibility in cats. Virus replicated in upper respiratory tract and lungs, followed by systemic dissemination and hematogenous neuroinvasion. Virus crossed blood-brain barrier by infecting endothelial cells, spreading to astrocytes and neurons, causing multifocal encephalitis. B3.13 caused rapid onset with efficient shedding and cat-to-cat transmission. D1.1 caused protracted disease with lower shedding and no transmissibility. High-level viremia and virus shedding in nasal, oral, and fecal secretions detected.
papers.annotation: Molecular mechanism of H5N1 neuroinvasion via endothelial cell infection and BBB crossing in mammalian host. Genotype-dependent pathogenesis with implications for cross-species transmission risk.

papers.title: Infection and transmission dynamics of bovine and human influenza A H5N1 viruses in mouse and hamster models
papers.abstract: Bovine-derived and human-derived H5N1 viruses compared for infection and transmission dynamics in mouse and hamster models. Characterizes pathogenesis, viral shedding patterns, and transmission efficiency across mammalian species.
papers.annotation: Comparative cross-species pathogenesis of bovine vs human H5N1 isolates in mammalian models. Relevant to understanding host adaptation but limited molecular interaction detail from available abstract.

papers.title: Optimizing an avian influenza vaccine using a novel Bacterial Enzymatic Combinatorial Chemistry (BECC) TLR4 adjuvant
papers.abstract: Evaluates BECC470s, a synthetic TLR4 adjuvant, for enhancing immunogenicity and protective efficacy of recombinant H5 hemagglutinin (rHA) vaccination in murine models.
papers.annotation: Vaccine adjuvant development targeting TLR4. Limited direct HPMI mechanism relevance.

papers.title: IFN-brite: A novel ISG15-based interferon reporter
papers.abstract: Novel ISG15-based reporter (IFN-brite) preferentially sensitive to IFN-beta. Detects IFN signaling in most brain cell types and in stromal and hematopoietic cells in the lung after influenza A infection. Highly sensitive reporter for IFN responses across multiple cell types.
papers.annotation: ISG15-based IFN reporter methodology paper. Useful tool for studying innate immune response to IAV but not a direct HPMI mechanism discovery.

papers.title: Influenza hemagglutinin subtypes have different sequence constraints despite sharing extremely similar structures
papers.abstract: Approximately 50% of HA sites display substantially diverged preferences for different amino acids across HA subtypes. Despite extremely similar structures, different HA subtypes are subject to distinct sequence constraints.
papers.annotation: Structural biology/evolutionary analysis of HA. Interesting for understanding viral protein evolution but not focused on host-pathogen molecular interactions.

papers.title: FluNexus: a versatile web platform for antigenic prediction and visualization of influenza A viruses
papers.abstract: Web platform for antigenic prediction and visualization of influenza A viruses. Computational tool for analyzing antigenic properties.
papers.annotation: Computational tool for influenza antigenic analysis. Not HPMI-focused.
